---
title: "Chapter 5 Integers: Function Summary"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Overview

This document reflects the current state of [HoTTRijke/chapter5_ints.lean](../HoTTRijke/chapter5_ints.lean).

It provides:
- A brief summary of each function.
- Function signatures, shown with backticks for readability.
- A separate section for possible issues.
- A separate section for duplicate or very similar functions.

# Structure

The Lean file is organized into:
- Namespace Naturals: algebraic equalities for myN addition and multiplication.
- Namespace Integers: algebraic equalities and helper laws for myZ addition and multiplication.

# Namespace Naturals

| Function | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| right_one_add_N |`(a : myN) → myAdd a _1 ≡ myN.succ a`| Right add-one law for naturals. |
| left_one_add_N |`(a : myN) → myAdd _1 a ≡ myN.succ a`| Left add-one law for naturals. |
| right_successor_law_add |`(a b : myN) → myAdd a (myN.succ b) ≡ myN.succ (myAdd a b)`| Successor rule in the right argument of addition. |
| left_successor_law_add |`(a b : myN) → myAdd (myN.succ a) b ≡ myN.succ (myAdd a b)`| Successor rule in the left argument of addition. |
| add_associative |`(a b c : myN) → myAdd (myAdd a b) c ≡ myAdd a (myAdd b c)`| Associativity of natural addition. |
| add_commutative |`(a b : myN) → myAdd a b ≡ myAdd b a`| Commutativity of natural addition. |
| mult_one_left |`(a : myN) → myMult _1 a ≡ a`| Left multiplicative unit for naturals. |
| mult_one_right |`(a : myN) → myMult a _1 ≡ a`| Right multiplicative unit for naturals. |
| mult_successor_right |`(a b : myN) → myMult a (myN.succ b) ≡ myAdd (myMult a b) a`| Right successor law for multiplication. |
| myAdd_right |`(a b : myN) → myN`| Helper that swaps arguments of myAdd (returns myAdd b a). |
| mult_successor_left |`(a b : myN) → myMult (myN.succ a) b ≡ myAdd (myMult a b) b`| Left successor law for multiplication. |
| mult_commutative |`(a b : myN) → (myMult a b) ≡ (myMult b a)`| Commutativity of natural multiplication. |
| mult_distributive_left |`(a b c : myN) → (a × (b + c)) ≡ ((a × b) + (a × c))`| Left distributivity of multiplication over addition. |
| mult_distributive_right |`(a b c : myN) → ((a + b) × c) ≡ (a × c) + (b × c)`| Right distributivity of multiplication over addition. |
| mult_associative |`(a b c : myN) → ((a × b) × c) ≡ (a × (b × c))`| Associativity of natural multiplication. |

# Namespace Integers

| Function | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| right_equiv_add |`(x : myZ) → (p : a ≡ b) → myAddZ x a ≡ myAddZ x b`| Congruence of integer addition in the right argument. |
| left_equiv_add |`(x : myZ) → (p : a ≡ b) → myAddZ a x ≡ myAddZ b x`| Congruence of integer addition in the left argument. |
| succ_pred_elim |`(k : myZ) → succZ (predZ k) ≡ k`| Successor after predecessor cancels. |
| pred_succ_elim |`(k : myZ) → predZ (succZ k) ≡ k`| Predecessor after successor cancels. |
| pred_succ_symm |`(z : myZ) → pred_succ_elim z • myEq_symm (succ_pred_elim z)`| Composite witness relating the two cancellation lemmas. |
| succ_pred_symm |`(z : myZ) → succ_pred_elim z • myEq_symm (pred_succ_elim z)`| Symmetric companion of pred_succ_symm. |
| right_add_zero |`(x : myZ) → (myAddZ x Zzero) ≡ x`| Right additive identity for integers. |
| zero_add_left_neg |`(n : myN) → (Zzero + Sum.inl n) ≡ Sum.inl n`| Left additive identity specialized to negative integers. |
| zero_add_left_pos |`(n : myN) → (Zzero + Sum.inr (Sum.inr n)) ≡ Sum.inr (Sum.inr n)`| Left additive identity specialized to positive integers. |
| zero_add_left_Z |`(a : myZ) → (Zzero + a) ≡ a`| Full left additive identity for integers. |
| addNtoZ_left_pred_law |`(n : myN) → (z : myZ) → addNaturalToZ n (predZ z) ≡ predZ (addNaturalToZ n z)`| Moves predZ across repeated natural addition into integers. |
| subNfromZ_left_pred_law |`(n : myN) → (z : myZ) → subtractNaturalFromZ n (predZ z) ≡ predZ (subtractNaturalFromZ n z)`| Moves predZ across repeated natural subtraction from integers. |
| addNtoZ_left_succ_law |`(n : myN) → (z : myZ) → addNaturalToZ n (succZ z) ≡ succZ (addNaturalToZ n z)`| Moves succZ across repeated natural addition into integers. |
| subNfromZ_left_succ_law |`(n : myN) → (z : myZ) → subtractNaturalFromZ n (succZ z) ≡ succZ (subtractNaturalFromZ n z)`| Moves succZ across repeated natural subtraction from integers. |
| left_pred_law |`(x y : myZ) → myAddZ (predZ x) y ≡ predZ (myAddZ x y)`| predZ commutes through the left addend. |
| right_pred_law |`(x y : myZ) → myAddZ x (predZ y) ≡ predZ (myAddZ x y)`| predZ commutes through the right addend. |
| right_succ_law |`(x y : myZ) → myAddZ x (succZ y) ≡ succZ (myAddZ x y)`| succZ commutes through the right addend. |
| left_succ_law |`(x y : myZ) → myAddZ (succZ x) y ≡ succZ (myAddZ x y)`| succZ commutes through the left addend. |
| addNtoZ_associative |`(n : myN) → (x y : myZ) → addNaturalToZ n (myAddZ x y) ≡ myAddZ x (addNaturalToZ n y)`| Associativity-style transport law for addNaturalToZ over integer addition. |
| subNfromZ_associative |`(n : myN) → (x y : myZ) → subtractNaturalFromZ n (myAddZ x y) ≡ myAddZ x (subtractNaturalFromZ n y)`| Associativity-style transport law for subtractNaturalFromZ over integer addition. |
| left_add_one_toZpos |`(n : myN) → myAddZ (Sum.inr (Sum.inr myN.one)) (Sum.inr (Sum.inr n)) ≡ succZ (Sum.inr (Sum.inr n))`| Left +1 law, positive case. |
| left_add_one_toZneg |`(n : myN) → myAddZ (Sum.inr (Sum.inr myN.one)) (Sum.inl n) ≡ succZ (Sum.inl n)`| Left +1 law, negative case. |
| left_sub_one_toZpos |`(n : myN) → myAddZ (Sum.inl myN.one) (Sum.inr (Sum.inr n)) ≡ predZ (Sum.inr (Sum.inr n))`| Left -1 law, positive case. |
| left_sub_one_toZneg |`(n : myN) → myAddZ (Sum.inl myN.one) (Sum.inl n) ≡ predZ (Sum.inl n)`| Left -1 law, negative case. |
| left_add_one |`(z : myZ) → myAddZ (Sum.inr (Sum.inr myN.one)) z ≡ succZ z`| Uniform left +1 law over all integer constructors. |
| left_sub_one |`(z : myZ) → myAddZ (Sum.inl myN.one) z ≡ predZ z`| Uniform left -1 law over all integer constructors. |
| addNtoZ_commutative |`(n : myN) → (z : myZ) → addNaturalToZ n z ≡ myAddZ (Sum.inr (Sum.inr n)) z`| Rewrites addNaturalToZ as integer addition by +n. |
| subNfromZ_commutative |`(n : myN) → (z : myZ) → subtractNaturalFromZ n z ≡ myAddZ (Sum.inl n) z`| Rewrites subtractNaturalFromZ as integer addition by -n. |
| addNtoZ_inverse |`(n : myN) → addNaturalToZ n (Sum.inl n) ≡ Zzero`| +n applied to -n yields zero. |
| subNfromZ_inverse |`(n : myN) → subtractNaturalFromZ n (Sum.inr (Sum.inr n)) ≡ Zzero`| Subtracting n from +n yields zero. |
| multNatWithZero |`(n : myN) → multNaturalWithZ Zzero n ≡ Zzero`| Zero annihilates natural-scalar integer multiplication. |
| multNatWithOne |`(n : myN) → multNaturalWithZ (Sum.inr (Sum.inr myN.one)) n ≡ Sum.inr (Sum.inr n)`| +1 is unit for natural-scalar integer multiplication. |
| add_commutative_Z |`(a b : myZ) → (a + b) ≡ (b + a)`| Integer addition commutativity theorem, currently partial (uses sorry). |
| add_associative_Z |`(a b c : myZ) → (a + (b + c)) ≡ ((a + b) + c)`| Integer addition associativity theorem, placeholder (sorry). |
| add_right_inverse |`(a : myZ) → (a + (negative a)) ≡ Zzero`| Right additive inverse theorem, placeholder (sorry). |
| add_left_inverse |`(a : myZ) → ((negative a) + a) ≡ Zzero`| Left additive inverse theorem, placeholder (sorry). |
| mult_right_zero |`(a : myZ) → (a × Zzero) ≡ Zzero`| Right zero law for integer multiplication, placeholder (sorry). |
| mult_left_zero |`(a : myZ) → (Zzero × a) ≡ Zzero`| Left zero law for integer multiplication, placeholder (sorry). |
| mult_right_unit |`(a : myZ) → (a × _1) ≡ a`| Right unit law for integer multiplication, placeholder (sorry). |
| mult_left_unit |`(a : myZ) → (_1 × a) ≡ a`| Left unit law for integer multiplication, placeholder (sorry). |
| mult_commutative |`(a b : myZ) → (a × b) ≡ (b × a)`| Integer multiplication commutativity theorem, placeholder (sorry). |
| mult_associative |`(a b c : myZ) → ((a × b) × c) ≡ a × (b × c)`| Integer multiplication associativity theorem, placeholder (sorry). |
| add_mult_right_distributive |`(a b c : myZ) → ((a + b) × c) ≡ ((a × c) + (b × c))`| Right distributivity theorem, placeholder (sorry). |
| add_mult_left_distributive |`(a b c : myZ) → (a × (b + c) × c) ≡ ((a × b) + (a × c))`| Left distributivity-shaped theorem, placeholder (sorry). |

# Possible Issues

1. Multiple placeholders remain in namespace Integers using sorry.
2. Statement shape of add_mult_left_distributive includes an extra trailing × c on the left side:`(a × (b + c) × c)`; this differs from standard left distributivity.
3. The declaration variable (U : Unit) appears unused.
4. #check myAdd is a debugging check and may be unnecessary in final coursework code.
5. Names addNtoZ_commutative and subNfromZ_commutative are potentially misleading: they express rewrite/equivalence with myAddZ, not commutativity between two independent operands.

# Duplicate and Similar Functions

## Potential Duplicates by Purpose

1. left_add_one_toZpos and left_add_one_toZneg are case-split helpers consolidated by left_add_one.
2. left_sub_one_toZpos and left_sub_one_toZneg are case-split helpers consolidated by left_sub_one.
3. zero_add_left_neg and zero_add_left_pos are case-split helpers consolidated by zero_add_left_Z.

## Very Similar (Dual) Pairs

1. succ_pred_elim and pred_succ_elim are dual cancellation theorems.
2. pred_succ_symm and succ_pred_symm have nearly identical structure with reversed composition order.
3. addNtoZ_left_pred_law and subNfromZ_left_pred_law are structural analogs.
4. addNtoZ_left_succ_law and subNfromZ_left_succ_law are structural analogs.
5. addNtoZ_associative and subNfromZ_associative are structural analogs.

## Cross-Namespace Name Reuse

1. mult_commutative exists in both namespace Naturals and namespace Integers (different domains and signatures).
2. mult_associative exists in both namespace Naturals and namespace Integers (different domains and signatures).

# Notes

- This summary is aligned with the current file content in [HoTTRijke/chapter5_ints.lean](../HoTTRijke/chapter5_ints.lean).
- Notation in the Lean source uses + and × inside local namespaces for readability of theorem statements.
